seo.title: 100 Ruby on Rails resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Ruby on Rails resources for engineers.

content.intro: The Ruby on Rails ecosystem has evolved into 'The One Person Framework' with the release of Rails 8, prioritizing self-hosting via Kamal and removing heavy dependencies like Redis for job processing. This resource guide focuses on the practical implementation of Rails 8 defaults, Hotwire patterns for modern frontends, and the integration of LLMs into Active Job pipelines.

items.title: Turbo Frames for Partial Page Updates
items.description: Isolate page segments to update content without a full reload. Use the 'src' attribute for lazy-loading heavy components like dashboards.

items.title: Turbo Streams over WebSockets
items.description: Broadcast model changes to all connected users using ActionCable. Use 'broadcast_replace_to' in models to trigger UI updates automatically.

items.title: StimulusJS State Management
items.description: Use Stimulus Values and Classes APIs to manage toggle states and CSS transitions without writing custom event listeners.

items.title: ViewComponent for UI Encapsulation
items.description: Move complex logic out of partials and into Ruby classes. Use GitHub's ViewComponent library for unit-testable UI units.

items.title: Turbo Morphing
items.description: Enable 'data-turbo-action="morph"' for smoother page transitions that preserve scroll position and input focus during updates.

items.title: Tailwind CSS JIT Integration
items.description: Configure tailwind.config.js to scan .rb and .erb files. Use the 'tailwindcss-rails' gem to manage the standalone CLI.

items.title: Phlex for Ruby-Based Views
items.description: An alternative to ERB that uses pure Ruby objects for views, offering significant performance gains for high-traffic pages.

items.title: Stimulus Use Hooks
items.description: Implement 'stimulus-use' for common patterns like click-outside, intersection observers, and debouncing in controllers.

items.title: Custom Turbo Stream Actions
items.description: Extend Turbo to handle non-standard DOM operations like triggering client-side logging or updating browser history.

items.title: Importmap-rails Management
items.description: Manage JavaScript dependencies without Node.js. Use 'bin/importmap pin' to add libraries directly from CDNs like JSPM.

items.title: Kamal Deployment Configuration
items.description: Deploy Rails applications to any VPS using Docker. Configure 'config/deploy.yml' with server IPs and environment secrets.

items.title: Solid Queue for Background Jobs
items.description: The new Rails 8 default job runner. Move away from Redis and use your PostgreSQL or MySQL database for persistence.

items.title: Solid Cache for Database Caching
items.description: Store cache fragments in the database instead of RAM. Ideal for keeping larger amounts of data cached at a lower cost.

items.title: Thruster HTTP/2 Proxy
items.description: Use the Thruster sidecar with Kamal to provide HTTP/2 support and asset compression without a complex Nginx setup.

items.title: Mission Control Jobs
items.description: Install the 'mission_control-jobs' engine to get a built-in dashboard for monitoring and retrying Solid Queue jobs.

items.title: Prop: Rate Limiting
items.description: Implement the 'prop' gem to prevent brute force attacks and API abuse by defining throttle strategies in an initializer.

items.title: Healthcheck Endpoint Configuration
items.description: Configure 'up' endpoints for Kamal or Kubernetes to monitor DB, Redis, and Job runner availability via 'rails-healthcheck'.

items.title: Zero-Downtime DB Migrations
items.description: Use the 'strong_migrations' gem to detect and block unsafe operations like adding columns with defaults on large tables.

items.title: Kamal Accessory Management
items.description: Define databases and search engines as 'accessories' in deploy.yml to manage their lifecycle alongside the Rails app.

items.title: Multi-Environment Kamal Deploys
items.description: Utilize Kamal's destination feature to manage staging and production environments within a single configuration file.

items.title: Langchainrb Orchestration
items.description: Use the 'langchainrb' gem to build RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) pipelines within Rails service objects.

items.title: Pgvector for Semantic Search
items.description: Install the 'pgvector' extension on PostgreSQL and use the 'neighbor' gem to perform vector similarity searches on records.

items.title: Streaming LLM Responses
items.description: Use ActionController::Live to stream OpenAI or Anthropic responses to the frontend as they are generated.

items.title: Active Job for LLM Callbacks
items.description: Offload LLM API calls to background jobs to avoid blocking the web thread. Use Solid Queue for reliable retries on rate limits.

items.title: Structured Data with JSON Schema
items.description: Enforce LLM output formats by passing JSON schemas to the OpenAI API and parsing responses into Active Model objects.

items.title: Embedding Generation on Save
items.description: Use ActiveRecord callbacks to automatically update vector embeddings in a background job whenever a record is updated.

items.title: Token Usage Tracking
items.description: Implement a 'Usage' model to track tokens consumed per user or per request to manage LLM costs and billing.

items.title: Prompt Versioning
items.description: Store system prompts in YAML files or a dedicated database table to version and A/B test prompt performance.

items.title: Function Calling with Rails Tools
items.description: Expose Rails service objects as tools for LLMs, allowing models to query your database or trigger actions via API.

items.title: Local LLM Development with Ollama
items.description: Connect your Rails development environment to Ollama to test AI features locally without incurring API costs.

pro_tips: Use 'delegated_type' to handle polymorphism in models without the pitfalls of Single Table Inheritance (STI).
Replace 'Kaminari' with 'Pagy' for pagination to reduce memory allocation by up to 30% in high-traffic lists.
Adopt 'StandardRB' for linting to enforce the Ruby community's best practices with zero configuration overhead.
Utilize 'ActiveStorage::Representations' with libvips for significantly faster image processing compared to ImageMagick.
Run 'bin/rails notes' to track TODOs, FIXMEs, and OPTIMIZE tags across your codebase directly from the CLI.
